purpose: Expose startup, liveness, and readiness probe endpoints with dependency-aware checks and explicit degraded states for service deployment environments.

endpoints.description: Return readiness status based on declared dependencies.
Return startup status while initialization is still in progress.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Standard health probe endpoint provider for service deployments.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use for services that need explicit startup, liveness, and readiness endpoints for orchestrators and operators.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a substitute for metrics, deep diagnostics, or business workflow completion checks.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Define dependency checks. 2) Mount /health/live, /health/ready, and /health/startup. 3) Point deployment probes to the matching endpoints.

failure_modes.symptom: readiness returns degraded or fail while liveness remains ok
failure_modes.recovery: Restore the dependency and retry readiness checks

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Model probes separately: startup reflects initialization phase, liveness only checks process viability, readiness checks dependencies and returns 200 only when traffic can be served.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Using one endpoint for all probes can cause restart loops or traffic during warmup. Readiness should degrade without crashing the process.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Provide a probe registry where each check returns name, status, and detail, then map those to startup, liveness, or readiness responses.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not block liveness on optional dependencies and do not report ready before initialization completes.

test_cases.name: startup endpoint reports starting before init complete
